title: NIS2 Backup Management and Disaster Recovery Verification

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(c) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.17: NIS2 Backup Management and Disaster Recovery Verification.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.17' running on index=backup (backup software logs — Veeam, Commvault, Rubrik, AWS Backup), index=itsi_summary (service recovery KPIs), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=backup` (backup software logs — Veeam, Commvault, Rubrik, AWS Backup), `index=itsi_summary` (service recovery KPIs)
app: Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312), Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841)
spl:
index=backup sourcetype IN ("veeam:backup","commvault:job","rubrik:event","aws:backup") earliest=-7d
| eval job_status=lower(coalesce(status, result, job_result, state))
| eval success=if(match(job_status,"(?i)success|completed|ok"), 1, 0)
| eval failed=if(match(job_status,"(?i)fail|error|warning|missed"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(success) as successful, sum(failed) as failed, latest(_time) as last_backup by job_name, target_system
| eval days_since_backup=round((now()-last_backup)/86400, 1)
| eval backup_gap=if(days_since_backup > 1, "OVERDUE", "OK")
| where failed > 0 OR backup_gap="OVERDUE"
| sort - days_since_backup
| table target_system, job_name, successful, failed, days_since_backup, backup_gap
description: While UC-22.2.4 monitors live service health during crises, Article 21(2)(c) specifically requires backup management and disaster recovery capabilities. This use case tracks backup job success/failure, validates restore test completion, and monitors RTO/RPO target adherence — providing the operational evidence that NIS2 auditors specifically ask for: "show me your last successful restore test."
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes backup, itsi_summary and sourcetypes aws:backup, commvault:job, rubrik:event. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Forward backup software logs via syslog or HEC; (2) define RTO/RPO targets per NIS2-scoped service and validate against actual backup frequency; (3) alert on any failed backup for critical systems; (4) track restore test completion in a KV store (date, system, result, duration); (5) schedule monthly restore tests and report results; (6) include restore test evidence in Article 21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment (UC-22.2.9). Indexes required: `index=backup`, `index=itsi_summary`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:backup`, `sourcetype=commvault:job`, `sourcetype=rubrik:event`, `sourcetype=veeam:backup`.
visualization: Table (failed/overdue backups), Single value (backup success rate %), Bar chart (failures by system), Timeline (backup history).
knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312), Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=backup` (backup software logs — Veeam, Commvault, Rubrik, AWS Backup), `index=itsi_summary` (service recovery KPIs).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Forward backup software logs via syslog or HEC; (2) define RTO/RPO targets per NIS2-scoped service and validate against actual backup frequency; (3) alert on any failed backup for critical systems; (4) track restore test completion in a KV store (date, system, result, duration); (5) schedule monthly restore tests and report results; (6) include restore test evidence in Article 21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment (UC-22.2.9).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=backup sourcetype IN ("veeam:backup","commvault:job","rubrik:event","aws:backup") earliest=-7d
| eval job_status=lower(coalesce(status, result, job_result, state))
| eval success=if(match(job_status,"(?i)success|completed|ok"), 1, 0)
| eval failed=if(match(job_status,"(?i)fail|error|warning|missed"), 1, 0)
| stats sum(success) as successful, sum(failed) as failed, latest(_time) as last_backup by job_name, target_system
| eval days_since_backup=round((now()-last_backup)/86400, 1)
| eval backup_gap=if(days_since_backup > 1, "OVERDUE", "OK")
| where failed > 0 OR backup_gap="OVERDUE"
| sort - days_since_backup
| table target_system, job_name, successful, failed, days_since_backup, backup_gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Backup Management and Disaster Recovery Verification (Art. 21(2)(c))** — While UC-22.2.4 monitors live service health during crises, Article 21(2)(c) specifically requires backup management and disaster recovery capabilities. This use case tracks backup job success/failure, validates restore test completion, and monitors RTO/RPO target adherence — providing the operational evidence that NIS2 auditors specifically ask for: "show me your last successful restore test."

Documented **Data sources**: `index=backup` (backup software logs — Veeam, Commvault, Rubrik, AWS Backup), `index=itsi_summary` (service recovery KPIs). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Veeam App for Splunk (Splunkbase 7312), Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: backup.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=backup, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **job_status** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **success** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **failed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **job_name, target_system**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since_backup** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **backup_gap** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where failed > 0 OR backup_gap="OVERDUE"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: backup_gap, days_since_backup, failed, job_name, job_status, last_backup, success, successful.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with AWS CloudTrail or the AWS console for the same account, region, and time window, and with IAM or control-tower evidence if this search flags admin activity.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed/overdue backups), Single value (backup success rate %), Bar chart (failures by system), Timeline (backup history).
